close_reason: agents can invoke sq add

description: Alert when background agents finish: terminal bell, desktop notification, or status line update. Reference: EXPLORATION.md Open Questions
close_reason: not important

title: Research custom Roast cog implementation
description: Dig into Roast::Cog base class to understand what's needed for a custom 'human' cog that pauses workflow, queues item for review, waits for decision. Look at: Cog::Config, Cog::Input, Cog::Output, execution lifecycle.
notes:
## Research Findings

### Custom Cog Structure
- Subclass `Roast::Cog`
- Define `Config`, `Input`, `Output` inner classes
- Implement `execute(input) -> Output`
- Register via `use [:cog_name], from: 'path'` in workflow

### Input Lifecycle
1. `Input.new` (no args)
2. Input block sets values
3. `validate!` called
4. If invalid, `coerce(return_value)` called
5. `validate!` again
6. `execute(input)` called

### Blocking Mechanism
Cog runs in `Async::Task`. Options for human-in-the-loop:
- Queue + Signal (Async::Queue)
- File watch
- Socket/HTTP callback
- DRb or similar IPC

### Next Steps
- Prototype a minimal `human` cog
- Test blocking behavior with Async
- Design Sift ↔ Cog communication protocol
close_reason: Researched Roast cog extension points. Custom cogs: subclass Roast::Cog, define Config/Input/Output, implement execute(). Register via 'use' DSL. Runs in Async::Task - can block on Queue/Signal for human input.

title: Source navigation and drill-down views
description:
Navigate between multiple sources within a queue item and drill into details.

## Source Navigation
- `n` / `→` - Next source
- `p` / `←` - Previous source  
- Status bar shows: `[2/4] file: src/foo.rb`

## Type-Aware Rendering
Each source type has custom display:

**diff**: Colored +/- lines, line numbers, file header
**file**: Syntax highlighting (via Rouge or similar), line numbers
**transcript**: Dialog format with speaker labels, timestamps
**text**: Markdown rendering or plain text

## Drill-down Views
- `d` - Full file diff (all hunks for current file, not just this one)
- `t` - Full transcript (complete Claude session history)
- `f` - File browser (list all sources, jump to any)

## Implementation
- Extract rendering logic from ReviewLoop into Source renderers
- Each source type: `Sift::Source::Diff`, `Sift::Source::File`, etc.
- Common interface: `#render(width:, height:)` returns formatted string

Depends on: n89 (queue model defines source structure)

close_reason: Deferred: Current ReviewLoop class is simple enough without DSL. Add DSL when we need configurable workflows (different sources, custom analyze prompts, etc.)

title: Implement sift_queue cog for Roast
description:
Custom Roast cog that pushes items to Sift queue from within a workflow.

## Use Case
Workflow is analyzing code, finds something that needs human review mid-workflow:

```ruby
# In workflow.rb
agent!(:analyze) do
  # ... analysis prompt ...
end

sift_queue do
  sources [
    { type: 'text', content: agent!(:analyze).response },
    { type: 'diff', path: target }
  ]
  metadata workflow: 'analyze', step: 'initial'
end

# Workflow continues...
```

## Cog Implementation
```ruby
module Sift
  module Cogs
    class SiftQueue < Roast::Cog
      class Input
        attr_accessor :sources, :metadata, :queue_path
      end
      
      class Output
        attr_accessor :item_id
      end
      
      def execute(input)
        queue = Sift::Queue.new(input.queue_path || '.sift/queue.jsonl')
        item = queue.push(
          sources: input.sources,
          metadata: input.metadata
        )
        Output.new(item_id: item.id)
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Registration
```ruby
# In workflow
use [:sift_queue], from: 'sift/cogs'
```

## Difference from atu
- atu: Sift calls Roast, captures final output
- 6ep: Roast calls Sift mid-workflow to queue items

Both are needed:
- atu for "run this workflow and review results"
- 6ep for "workflow decides what needs human review"

## Dependencies
- n89: Queue API must exist
- atu: May share queue instance/configuration
close_reason: Already implemented as sift_output cog in atu. Same functionality: workflow pushes to Sift queue via cog.

title: Define minimal prototype scope
description: What's the smallest thing that proves the concept? Probably: hardcoded items, single agent (no parallel), basic approve/reject/revise, no persistence. Spec this out.
close_reason: Scope defined. See EXPLORATION.md 'Prototype Scope (v0)' section.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
## Prototype Scope - v0

### Goal
Prove the UX feels right. After 10 minutes of use, you're in flow - not fighting the tool.

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Fast feedback loop (item → decide → next feels snappy)
- [ ] Right information density (enough context, not overwhelming)
- [ ] Revision flow feels natural (conversational)
- [ ] Want to keep using it

### In Scope
- Hardcoded list of items (3-5 strings to review)
- Real Claude agent (single, sequential - no parallelism)
- Basic TUI: show item + response, hotkeys for actions
- Actions: approve, reject, revise (free-form text)
- Session continuity for revise (agent remembers context)
- In-memory only (no persistence)

### Explicitly Cut
- JSONL queue file
- Workflow DSL
- Parallel/background agents
- Persistence across runs
- Drill-down views (diff, transcript)
- Custom context hooks

### Tech Stack (tentative)
- Ruby script
- CLI::UI or TTY gems for prompts
- Claude CLI for agent (--resume for sessions)

### Test Scenario
Review 3-5 code snippets. For each:
1. See snippet + Claude's analysis
2. Decide: approve / reject / revise
3. If revise: type feedback, see continued response
4. Move to next

### Open Question
What items should we hardcode for testing? Options:
- Code snippets with intentional issues
- Git diff hunks from a real repo
- Simple strings to keep it minimal

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Decision: Test Data
Use real git diff hunks from an actual repo. This matches the original motivating use case and proves the UX with realistic content.

Could use:
- Hunks from a repo with recent changes
- Or generate some test changes specifically for this

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Decision: Diff Source
Use /Users/derek/.dotfiles repo for real git diff hunks.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Test Data Confirmed
Source: /Users/derek/.dotfiles (HEAD~3..HEAD)

5 diff hunks available:
1. ghostty config - new file, 1 line
2. raycast package.json - removed 13 lines (background/menubar commands)
3. raycast types - removed 8 lines (corresponding type defs)
4. configure script - changed stow alacritty → stow ghostty
5. zshrc - simplified SPIN conditional (removed 5 lines)

Good variety: new file, deletions, small edits.

title: Integrate Roast wrapper orchestration
description:
Sift orchestrates Roast workflows externally, captures output, queues results for human review.

## Orchestrator API
```ruby
orchestrator = Sift::Roast::Orchestrator.new(
  workflow: 'workflows/analyze.rb',
  queue: Sift::Queue.new('.sift/queue.jsonl')
)

# Run workflow, queue results
orchestrator.run(
  targets: ['src/foo.rb', 'src/bar.rb'],
  kwargs: { context: 'refactoring' }
)

# Run revision workflow with feedback
orchestrator.revise(
  item_id: 'abc123',
  feedback: 'Focus more on performance implications'
)
```

## Output Capture
Roast::Workflow.from_file returns void, so capture via:
- File-based: Workflow writes to temp file, orchestrator reads it
- Custom output cog: Workflow uses `sift_output` cog that writes to known location
- Alternatively: Modify Roast to return workflow result

## Queue Integration
When workflow completes:
1. Parse output (structured or free-form)
2. Create queue item with sources:
   - transcript: workflow conversation
   - text: workflow output/analysis
   - file/diff: original targets
3. Push to queue with metadata (workflow name, run timestamp)

## Revision Flow
1. Human provides feedback on queue item
2. Orchestrator runs revision workflow with:
   - Original context (from item metadata)
   - Human feedback (as kwarg)
   - Session continuity if supported
3. New/updated item appears in queue

## Dependencies
- n89: Queue must exist to push results
- Roast gem: Need to understand from_file API better
close_reason: Roast wrapper orchestrator: run() and revise() methods, sift_output custom cog for workflows to push to queue via SIFT_QUEUE_PATH env var

title: Add parallel/background agents
description: Run agent analysis in background while human reviews. Queue coordinates: when agent finishes, result appears in queue. Reference: EXPLORATION.md Architecture Diagram

title: Decide: Roast extension vs standalone
description: Three options: extend Roast with queue/input cogs, build on top of Roast, or standalone with cherry-picked ideas. Queue-driven model is fundamentally different from Roast's step-execution model.
close_reason: Decision: Standalone for prototype. Clean separation, simpler to iterate. Can integrate with Roast later if queue model proves valuable.

title: Understand Claude CLI session mechanics
description: How does claude --resume work? Does it fit our sticky-session-per-item model? What's the session ID format? Can we fork sessions?
close_reason: Mechanics verified. See EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics' section.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
## Findings: Claude CLI Session Mechanics

### Key Flags
- `--resume <session-id>` - Resume existing session by UUID
- `--fork-session` - When resuming, create NEW session ID (branch off)
- `--session-id <uuid>` - Use specific UUID for new session
- `-c, --continue` - Continue most recent session
- `--no-session-persistence` - Don't save to disk

### Session ID Format
- Standard UUID: `bf32670c-abf7-48d2-b5d2-7f29ae656aae`

### Getting Session ID
JSON output includes `session_id`:
```bash
echo "prompt" | claude -p --output-format json | jq -r '.session_id'
```

### Session Continuity (Verified)
```bash
# Create session
result=$(echo "Remember: 42" | claude -p --output-format json)
session=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.session_id')

# Resume it
echo "What number?" | claude -p --output-format json --resume "$session"
# Returns: 42
```

### Forking (Verified)
```bash
# Fork from base session - gets new ID, keeps context
echo "New prompt" | claude -p --output-format json --resume "$base" --fork-session
```
- Each fork gets unique session ID
- Context preserved from fork point
- Multiple forks from same base work independently

### Implications for Prototype
1. **Initial analysis**: Run prompt, capture session_id
2. **Revise flow**: Use `--resume <id> --fork-session` to continue with feedback
3. **Sticky sessions**: Store session_id per queue item
4. **No forking needed for simple revise**: Can just `--resume` without fork if we want linear conversation

### Open Question
Should revise fork (branch) or continue (linear)?
- Fork: Can try multiple revisions from same point
- Continue: Simpler, linear conversation per item

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
### Decision: Linear Continuation
Revise uses `--resume <id>` without fork. Simple linear conversation per item.

Forking available later if we want 'try multiple approaches' feature.

title: Persist queue to JSONL
description:
JSONL queue persistence with multi-source items.

## Queue Item Model
```json
{
  "id": "abc123",
  "status": "pending",
  "sources": [
    {"type": "diff", "path": "src/foo.rb", "content": "..."},
    {"type": "file", "path": "src/foo_test.rb"},
    {"type": "transcript", "session_id": "uuid", "content": "..."},
    {"type": "text", "content": "Review notes here"}
  ],
  "metadata": {},
  "session_id": null,
  "created_at": "...",
  "updated_at": "..."
}
```

## Source Types
- **diff**: Git hunk with colored +/- display
- **file**: Path reference (lazy load) or inline content
- **transcript**: Claude conversation history
- **text**: Freeform markdown/plain text

## Status Values
- pending, in_progress, approved, rejected, failed

## Queue API (Ruby)
```ruby
queue = Sift::Queue.new('.sift/queue.jsonl')
queue.push(sources: [...], metadata: {...})
queue.each_pending { |item| ... }
queue.update(id, status: :approved)
queue.filter(status: :pending)
```

## File Format
- One JSON object per line (JSONL)
- Append-only writes, periodic compaction
- Git-friendly (merge conflicts rare)
close_reason: Queue foundation implemented: Item/Source structs, JSONL persistence, full API (push/update/find/filter/count/remove/clear), status helpers, validation

title: Add session continuity for revise
description: When user types revision feedback, call claude --resume <session_id> with the feedback. Update stored session_id. Show new response. Reference: EXPLORATION.md 'Claude CLI Session Mechanics'
close_reason: Already implemented in ReviewLoop#revise_analysis: resumes session with --resume flag, updates stored session_id, shows revised analysis

title: Research Sift as Roast wrapper gem
description: Explore pattern where Sift orchestrates Roast workflows externally. Sift manages queue/UI, calls roast execute programmatically, captures output, handles human review loop. May not need custom cog at all.
close_reason: Wrapper approach: Sift calls Roast::Workflow.from_file externally, queues results for review, runs separate workflows for revisions. Simpler than custom cog, loses session continuity. Recommend starting here per Sam's advice.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
## Detailed API Findings (2026-02-04)

### Calling Workflows Programmatically

```ruby
require 'roast'

params = Roast::WorkflowParams.new(
  ["target1"],     # targets - array of strings
  [:flag],         # args - array of symbols  
  { key: "value" } # kwargs - hash
)

Roast::Workflow.from_file("/path/to/workflow.rb", params)
```

### Key Files
- `lib/roast/workflow.rb:14-22` - `from_file` entry point
- `lib/roast/workflow_params.rb` - params container
- `lib/roast/workflow_context.rb` - runtime context

### Session Continuity for Revisions

Agent cog supports session continuation:

```ruby
execute do
  agent(:analysis) { "Analyze: #{diff}" }
  agent(:revision) do |my|
    my.session = agent!(:analysis).session  # Continue conversation!
    "Here's what I expected: #{feedback}"
  end
end
```

### Agent Output Properties
- `response` - final text
- `session` - session ID for continuation
- `text`, `lines`, `json`, `json!` - convenience accessors

### Gap: Getting Results Back

`from_file` returns void. Options:
1. File-based output - workflow writes to temp file
2. Extend Roast - return `execution_manager.final_output`
3. Use Claude provider directly

title: CLI queue management
description:
Full-featured CLI for queue operations:

## Commands
- `sift queue add` - Add item with sources
- `sift queue edit <id>` - Edit item fields via flags  
- `sift queue list` - List items (filterable by status)
- `sift queue show <id>` - Display item details
- `sift queue rm <id>` - Remove item

## Add command flags
```
--diff <path>        Add diff source (can repeat)
--file <path>        Add file source (can repeat)
--text <string>      Add text source (can repeat)
--transcript <path>  Add transcript source
--stdin <type>       Read source from stdin (diff|file|text)
--metadata <json>    Attach metadata
```

## Edit command flags
```
--add-diff <path>    Add diff source
--add-file <path>    Add file source
--rm-source <index>  Remove source by index
--set-status <s>     Change status
--set-metadata <json>
```

## Examples
```bash
# Add diff from stdin
git diff src/foo.rb | sift queue add --stdin diff

# Add item with multiple sources
sift queue add --diff src/foo.rb --file src/foo_test.rb --text 'Review this refactor'

# Edit existing item
sift queue edit abc123 --add-file README.md --set-metadata '{"priority": 1}'
```

Depends on: n89 (queue foundation)
close_reason: CLI queue management: add/edit/list/show/rm subcommands, stdin support, JSON output, repeatable source flags. Refactored exe/sift for subcommands.
